methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Dusty Robotics is a Series B-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $70M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—in the robotics sector at similar stages. Key private comparables include 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $18M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $34.2M. Peer-median benchmarking across the 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This wide range reflects material heterogeneity within the peer set, particularly the outsized valuations of 1X Technologies relative to mid-market peers like Fairmat and Collaborative Robotics.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $29M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $555M. The wide dispersion between the stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects fundamental uncertainty regarding where Dusty Robotics ranks within the robotics sector by market opportunity, traction, and risk profile. Resolution of this range depends on detailed assessment of revenue trajectory, unit economics, and technological differentiation relative to the peer cohort.
